gaps.title: Path traversal in file upload endpoint
gaps.description: The upload endpoint uses f.filename directly in os.path.join(DOCS_DIR, f.filename). A malicious filename like '../../config.py' or '../../.env' would resolve outside DOCS_DIR, allowing an attacker to overwrite any file on the server the process has write access to, including .env (leaking API keys) or app.py (code injection).
gaps.root_cause: No filename sanitization before constructing the file path. os.path.join does not prevent traversal when the second argument contains '..'.
gaps.impact: Arbitrary file write on the server. Attacker can overwrite .env to inject their own API keys, overwrite app.py for RCE, or destroy the database.
gaps.fix: Sanitize filenames: `from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename` or manually: `safe_name = os.path.basename(f.filename); path = os.path.join(DOCS_DIR, safe_name)`. Also validate the resolved path starts with DOCS_DIR: `assert os.path.realpath(path).startswith(os.path.realpath(DOCS_DIR))`.

gaps.title: Path traversal in document preview via resolve_doc_path
gaps.description: resolve_doc_path() tries Path(source_file) as a direct candidate (L27). If a source_file value like '/etc/passwd' or 'C:\Windows\System32\config\SAM' is passed, it will resolve to that path. The backend /api/document-preview endpoint and Streamlit render_document_preview_html both use this function. An attacker can read arbitrary files on the server.
gaps.root_cause: resolve_doc_path includes Path(source_file) as a raw fallback candidate without restricting to the documents directory.
gaps.impact: Arbitrary file read. Attacker can read .env (API keys, email passwords), database file, source code, or OS files.
gaps.fix: Remove the `Path(source_file)` fallback. Ensure all candidates are within DOCS_DIR: `resolved = candidate.resolve(); if not str(resolved).startswith(str(Path(DOCS_DIR).resolve())): continue`.

gaps.title: XSS via unsanitized user input in Streamlit HTML
gaps.description:
User message content is injected directly into HTML: `f'
{msg["content"]}
'` with unsafe_allow_html=True. A user can type `` or `` to execute arbitrary JavaScript in other users' browsers.
gaps.root_cause: msg['content'] is user-controlled text inserted into raw HTML without escaping. Streamlit's unsafe_allow_html=True disables all XSS protections.
gaps.impact: Cross-site scripting. In shared/multi-user deployments, attacker can steal session cookies, redirect users, or inject phishing content.
gaps.fix:
Escape user content before injection: `import html; safe = html.escape(msg['content'])` then use `f'
{safe}
'`. Alternatively, use st.chat_message() which handles escaping.

gaps.title: Leave can be approved multiple times, deducting balance repeatedly
gaps.description: approve_leave() fetches the leave record without checking its current status. The UPDATE on L136 sets status='Approved' unconditionally. If called twice with the same leave_id, balance is deducted twice. Similarly, an already-rejected leave can be re-approved.
gaps.root_cause: No WHERE clause checking `status='Pending'` in the SELECT or UPDATE queries.
gaps.impact: Leave balance can go negative through repeated approvals. Data integrity of leave_balances is compromised.
gaps.fix: Change L127 to: `SELECT employee_id, leave_type, start_date, end_date FROM leaves WHERE id=? AND status='Pending'`. If row is None, return 'Leave is not in Pending status'. Same fix for reject_leave (L165).

gaps.title: Leave balance can go negative — no minimum check
gaps.description: approve_leave deducts days from leave_balances without checking if days_remaining >= days. A 10-day leave approval when only 2 days remain results in days_remaining = -8.
gaps.root_cause: No validation of sufficient balance before deduction.
gaps.impact: Negative leave balances. Business rules violated. Could cause downstream issues in analytics and reports.
gaps.fix: Before deducting, check balance: `bal = c.execute('SELECT days_remaining FROM leave_balances WHERE employee_id=? AND leave_type=?', (emp_id, l_type)).fetchone(); if not bal or bal[0] < days: return f'Insufficient balance: {bal[0]} days available, {days} requested'`.

gaps.title: CORS allows all origins in production
gaps.description: allow_origins=['*'] permits any website to make API requests. Combined with no authentication, any malicious site can make requests to /api/chat, /api/upload, or /api/ingest on behalf of any user who visits their page.
gaps.root_cause: Wildcard CORS origin with no authentication layer.
gaps.impact: CSRF-like attacks. A malicious website can trigger document ingestion, send emails via the agent, or exfiltrate HR data through the chat endpoint.
gaps.fix: Restrict origins: `allow_origins=['http://localhost:3000']` for dev. For production, set to the actual frontend domain. Add authentication middleware.

gaps.title: Missing comma in policy_keywords creates wrong keyword
gaps.description: Line 46: `"solicit" "external work"` — Python concatenates adjacent string literals, producing `"solicitexternal work"` instead of two separate keywords `"solicit"` and `"external work"`. This means neither 'solicit' nor 'external work' will individually match moonlighting queries.
gaps.root_cause: Typo: missing comma between two string literals in a list.
gaps.impact: Moonlighting policy queries containing 'solicit' or 'external work' won't be correctly boosted. Retrieval quality degraded for moonlighting-related questions.
gaps.fix: Change line 46 to: `"moonlighting": ["moonlight", "outside work", "dual employment", "side job", "covenants", "compete", "solicit", "external work"],`

gaps.title: Debug diagnostic code left in production
gaps.description: Lines 200-219 contain a diagnostic block that re-imports get_all_tools and re-loads all 17 tools at module load time just to print them. This runs on every import of graph.py, doubling tool initialization time and printing debug output to stderr/stdout in production.
gaps.root_cause: Debugging code not removed before deployment.
gaps.impact: Doubled startup time for tool loading. Debug output clutters server logs. Module-level `tools` variable is overwritten (L208) but the original tools bound to LLM (L19-L27) remain, causing potential confusion.
gaps.fix: Delete lines 200-219 entirely. Also remove debug print statements on L22, L126-128, L150.

gaps.title: No date validation in apply_leave
gaps.description: apply_leave accepts any string for start_date and end_date without validation. Issues: (1) start_date > end_date produces negative day counts, (2) invalid date formats cause uncaught ValueError in approve_leave's datetime.strptime, (3) past dates are accepted, (4) overlapping leave periods for the same employee aren't detected.
gaps.root_cause: No input validation on date parameters before database insertion.
gaps.impact: Invalid leave records in database. approve_leave will crash on malformed dates. Negative day counts possible.
gaps.fix: Add validation at top of apply_leave: `try: d1 = datetime.strptime(start_date, '%Y-%m-%d'); d2 = datetime.strptime(end_date, '%Y-%m-%d'); except ValueError: return 'Dates must be YYYY-MM-DD format'; if d1 > d2: return 'start_date must be before end_date'; if d1.date() < datetime.now().date(): return 'Cannot apply for past dates'`.

gaps.title: Source deduplication disabled in Streamlit but active in FastAPI
gaps.description: Line 213: `# sources = deduplicate_sources(sources)` is commented out. The FastAPI backend (api.py L103) has deduplication enabled. This means Streamlit users see duplicate source citations while React users don't.
gaps.root_cause: Intentional or accidental commenting-out of deduplication call.
gaps.impact: Inconsistent behavior between frontends. Streamlit shows duplicate source cards cluttering the preview panel.
gaps.fix: Uncomment L213: `sources = deduplicate_sources(sources)`

gaps.title: FastAPI /api/chat has no error handling — 500s leak stack traces
gaps.description: The chat endpoint calls graph.stream() without try/except. Any exception (OpenAI API error, ChromaDB connection failure, tool crash) results in an unhandled 500 with full stack trace in the response, potentially exposing internal paths, API keys in config, and code structure.
gaps.root_cause: No exception handler wrapping the agent execution pipeline.
gaps.impact: Information disclosure via stack traces. Server errors give no user-friendly message. API consumers see raw Python tracebacks.
gaps.fix: Wrap in try/except: `try: ... except Exception as e: raise HTTPException(500, detail='An error occurred processing your request. Please try again.')`.

gaps.title: No validation or error for missing API key
gaps.description: OPENAI_API_KEY = os.getenv('AI_KEY') or os.getenv('OPENAI_API_KEY') — if neither env var is set, this silently becomes None. ChatOpenAI is initialized with api_key=None (graph.py L24) and will fail with a confusing error on the first query, not at startup.
gaps.root_cause: No startup validation that required configuration values are present.
gaps.impact: Application starts successfully but crashes with unclear error on first user query. Debugging is difficult for new developers.
gaps.fix: Add validation in config.py after loading: `if not OPENAI_API_KEY: raise ValueError('Missing API key. Set AI_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY in .env file.')`.

gaps.title: Missing critical dependencies in requirements.txt
gaps.description: Several packages used in the codebase are not listed in requirements.txt: pdfplumber (utils/document_viewer.py, tools/retrieval.py), fastapi (backend/api.py), uvicorn (backend/api.py), langchain-text-splitters (ingest_docs.py). Installing from requirements.txt alone will fail to run the application.
gaps.root_cause: Dependencies added during development without updating requirements.txt.
gaps.impact: Fresh installations fail. `pip install -r requirements.txt` produces ImportError on first run.
gaps.fix:
Add missing deps: `pdfplumber
fastapi
uvicorn[standard]
langchain-text-splitters` to requirements.txt.

gaps.title: Leave day calculation includes weekends and holidays
gaps.description: days = (d2 - d1).days + 1 counts calendar days, not business days. A Monday-to-Sunday leave (7 calendar days) deducts 7 days instead of 5 working days.
gaps.root_cause: Simple date arithmetic without accounting for weekends or company holidays.
gaps.impact: Employees are overcharged leave days for periods spanning weekends. Leave balances are incorrectly reduced.
gaps.fix: Use numpy.busday_count or manual weekend exclusion: `import numpy as np; days = int(np.busday_count(start, end)) + 1` or iterate and skip Saturday/Sunday.

gaps.title: No authentication or authorization on any API endpoint
gaps.description: All endpoints (/api/chat, /api/upload, /api/ingest, /api/documents, /api/document-preview) are publicly accessible without any authentication. Anyone with network access can upload files, trigger ingestion, query the LLM, or read documents.
gaps.root_cause: No auth middleware or API key validation implemented.
gaps.impact: Unauthorized access to all HR data, document uploads, and LLM queries. In a deployed environment, this is a complete security bypass.
gaps.fix: Add API key or JWT auth: `from fastapi.security import APIKeyHeader; api_key = APIKeyHeader(name='X-API-Key'); @app.middleware('http') async def auth(request, call_next): if request.headers.get('X-API-Key') != os.getenv('API_SECRET'): raise HTTPException(401); return await call_next(request)`.

gaps.title: ChromaDB collection deleted before ingestion — data loss on failure
gaps.description: The collection is deleted (L298) and recreated (L309) before any documents are processed. If ingestion fails midway (OOM, corrupt file, network error), the collection is left empty or partial. All previously indexed documents are lost.
gaps.root_cause: Destructive delete-then-recreate pattern without backup or transactional semantics.
gaps.impact: Complete loss of search functionality until re-ingestion succeeds. In production with large document sets, recovery could take significant time.
gaps.fix: Ingest into a temporary collection, then swap: `temp_name = 'hr_docs_v2_temp'; ... ingest into temp_name ...; client.delete_collection('hr_docs_v2'); # rename or recreate from temp`. Or backup before delete.

gaps.title: seed_db.py drops all tables unconditionally — production data loss risk
gaps.description: Running seed_db.py drops employees, leaves, leave_balances, onboarding_tasks, and employee_onboarding tables without any confirmation or environment check. Accidentally running in production destroys all data.
gaps.root_cause: No environment guard or confirmation prompt before destructive operations.
gaps.impact: Complete data loss of all employee records, leave history, and onboarding progress.
gaps.fix: Add guard: `if os.getenv('ALLOW_SEED', 'false').lower() != 'true': print('Set ALLOW_SEED=true to run. This DESTROYS all data.'); sys.exit(1)`. Or check for existing data: `if c.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employees').fetchone()[0] > 0: confirm = input('DB has data. Continue? (yes/no): '); if confirm != 'yes': sys.exit(0)`.

gaps.title: No version pinning — breaking changes risk
gaps.description: All dependencies are unpinned (e.g., 'langchain', 'chromadb', 'langgraph'). These packages have frequent breaking API changes. A fresh install may get incompatible versions.
gaps.root_cause: Dependencies listed without version constraints.
gaps.impact: Builds break unpredictably when upstream packages release breaking changes. Particularly risky for langchain/langgraph ecosystem which changes frequently.
gaps.fix:
Pin to known working versions: `streamlit==1.31.0
chromadb==0.4.22
langchain==0.1.20
langgraph==0.0.55
langchain-openai==0.1.7` etc. Generate with `pip freeze > requirements.txt`.

gaps.title: Error messages may expose sensitive information
gaps.description: The except block on L223-230 captures the full exception and displays it: `st.error(f'Error: {e}')` and stores it in messages. OpenAI errors can include API key fragments, ChromaDB errors include file paths, and SQLite errors include query text.
gaps.root_cause: Full exception message passed to UI without sanitization.
gaps.impact: Sensitive information (paths, partial API keys, query structures) may be displayed to the user.
gaps.fix: Log the full error server-side, show generic message to user: `import traceback; traceback.print_exc(); st.error('Something went wrong. Please try again.'); st.session_state.messages.append({'role':'assistant','content':'I encountered an error. Please try again.','sources':[]})`.

gaps.title: Document preview endpoint allows reading files outside documents dir
gaps.description: The /api/document-preview endpoint passes req.source_file to resolve_doc_path() which (as noted in GAP-002) can resolve to arbitrary paths. An attacker can POST {source_file: '/etc/passwd', start_line: 1, end_line: 100} to read system files.
gaps.root_cause: No input validation on source_file parameter. resolve_doc_path has an unsafe fallback.
gaps.impact: Arbitrary file read via API. Combined with no authentication (GAP-015), this is remotely exploitable.
gaps.fix: Validate source_file is a basename only: `if os.path.sep in req.source_file or '..' in req.source_file: raise HTTPException(400, 'Invalid filename')`. Fix resolve_doc_path per GAP-002.

gaps.title: Document ingestion blocks Streamlit UI thread
gaps.description: subprocess.run() on L73 runs ingest_docs.py synchronously. For large document sets with many PDFs, this can take minutes, during which the Streamlit tab is completely frozen with just a spinner.
gaps.root_cause: Synchronous subprocess call in the main Streamlit thread.
gaps.impact: UI becomes unresponsive during ingestion. Users may think the app crashed and refresh, losing state.
gaps.fix: Use subprocess.Popen for async execution with progress polling, or use st.status() with periodic checks: `proc = subprocess.Popen([...], stdout=subprocess.PIPE); while proc.poll() is None: time.sleep(1); st.spinner('...')`.

gaps.title: CrossEncoder reranker scores not normalized — scoring formula unreliable
gaps.description: The combined scoring (L196-203) assumes relevance_score, verification_score, and policy_match are in comparable ranges. But CrossEncoder scores (relevance_score) can be any real number (negative to positive), while verification_score is [0,1] and policy_match is binary. High/low CrossEncoder scores dominate the ranking unpredictably.
gaps.root_cause: No normalization of CrossEncoder scores before combining with other metrics.
gaps.impact: Ranking quality is inconsistent. Low-relevance documents with high CrossEncoder scores may be ranked above truly relevant ones.
gaps.fix: Normalize CrossEncoder scores to [0,1]: `from scipy.special import expit; chunk['relevance_score'] = float(expit(score))` or use min-max normalization across the batch.

gaps.title: apply_leave reports 'Notifications sent' even when email fails
gaps.description: _send_smtp_email returns an error string on failure, but apply_leave ignores the return value (L77-91). The final return on L93 always says 'Notifications sent' regardless of email delivery status.
gaps.root_cause: Return value of _send_smtp_email is not checked.
gaps.impact: User believes notifications were sent when emails actually failed (e.g., due to SMTP misconfiguration). Manager never receives the leave request.
gaps.fix: Capture and report email status: `emp_result = _send_smtp_email(...); mgr_result = _send_smtp_email(...); notify_status = 'Emails sent' if 'successfully' in emp_result else f'Email warning: {emp_result}'`.

gaps.title: SQLite connections not using context managers — leak on exception
gaps.description: All tool modules (employee_tool, leave_tool, onboarding_tool, analytics_tool) create connections with _get_conn() and manually call conn.close(). If an exception occurs between connect and close, the connection is never released.
gaps.root_cause: Manual connection management without try/finally or context manager (with statement).
gaps.impact: SQLite connection leaks under error conditions. While SQLite handles this more gracefully than other databases, it can lead to locked database files.
gaps.fix: Use context manager pattern: `def _get_conn(): return sqlite3.connect(DB_PATH)` then in each tool: `with _get_conn() as conn: c = conn.cursor(); ...` This auto-commits on success and auto-rolls-back on exception.

gaps.title: SentenceTransformer/CrossEncoder loading failure unhandled
gaps.description: SentenceTransformer(LOCAL_EMBEDDING_MODEL) on L25 will fail if the model isn't downloaded and there's no internet connection. _retriever is instantiated at module level (L208), so import of tools.retrieval crashes the entire application.
gaps.root_cause: No try/except around model loading. Module-level instantiation means failure is fatal.
gaps.impact: Application fails to start if embedding model download fails or model name is invalid. No graceful degradation.
gaps.fix: Wrap in try/except with clear error: `try: self.model = SentenceTransformer(LOCAL_EMBEDDING_MODEL) except Exception as e: raise RuntimeError(f'Failed to load embedding model {LOCAL_EMBEDDING_MODEL}. Run: python -c "from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer; SentenceTransformer(\'all-MiniLM-L6-v2\')"') from e`.

gaps.title: Full PDF loaded for every search query containing PDF sources
gaps.description: search_policies loads ALL pages of every matched PDF via pdfplumber.open() (L254-258) on each query. For a 500-page PDF, this means extracting text from all 500 pages even if only 2 pages matched. This happens synchronously during the agent's tool call.
gaps.root_cause: Loading full PDF content to support preview panel pagination, done eagerly during search rather than lazily on preview request.
gaps.impact: Significantly increased response time for queries hitting PDF sources. Memory spikes for large PDFs. Scale: O(total_pages × matched_files) per query.
gaps.fix: Load only matched pages during search. Move full-PDF loading to the preview endpoint/render function: `full_content_pages = [{'page': p, 'text': get_pdf_page_text(str(filepath), p)} for p in pages]`. Load remaining pages lazily in document_viewer.

gaps.title: Greeting detection could intercept legitimate HR queries
gaps.description: Exact single-word match after rstrip('!.,') means 'Hello' alone returns a canned greeting. But 'Hola!' also matches. Edge case: a user who types only 'Hi' expecting to start a conversation gets no agent functionality. More critically, the response 'How should I help you today?' has no sources/steps, so the preview panel shows stale data from a previous query.
gaps.root_cause: Greeting bypass skips the agent entirely, and the response lacks the structural fields (steps, sources) that the UI expects for proper state management.
gaps.impact: Minor: stale source previews after greeting. Could confuse users who follow up with 'as I was saying...' type queries.
gaps.fix: Keep greeting detection but clear stale state: After the greeting response message, explicitly set `st.session_state.current_sources = []`. Or route greetings through the agent which already declines irrelevant queries.

gaps.title: No recursion limit set on LangGraph — potential runaway tool loops
gaps.description: workflow.compile() on L197 uses LangGraph's default recursion_limit (typically 25). While not infinite, a pathological query could cause 25 agent→tools→agent cycles, consuming significant OpenAI credits and time (~25 API calls).
gaps.root_cause: No explicit recursion_limit parameter on compile().
gaps.impact: Excessive API costs and response time for edge-case queries that trigger repeated tool calls.
gaps.fix: Set explicit limit: `graph = workflow.compile(recursion_limit=5)`. Also add a check in agent_node if len(messages) > threshold.

gaps.title: Single corrupt document crashes entire ingestion pipeline
gaps.description: The main loop iterates over files and calls extractor functions. If any extractor raises an unhandled exception (e.g. corrupt PDF, locked file), the entire ingestion aborts. Documents processed before the failure are already in ChromaDB, but remaining ones are lost.
gaps.root_cause: No per-file try/except in the main ingestion loop.
gaps.impact: One corrupt file prevents all subsequent files from being indexed. Combined with GAP-016 (collection wiped first), this leaves a partially indexed state.
gaps.fix: Wrap each file in try/except: `try: file_chunks = extract_...(filepath) except Exception as e: print(f'ERROR: Skipping {filename}: {e}'); continue`.

gaps.title: Embeddings computed one at a time instead of in batch
gaps.description: Line 354: `model.encode(c['text']).tolist()` — each chunk is encoded individually in a for loop. SentenceTransformer.encode() accepts a list of strings and processes them in GPU batches, which is 10-50x faster.
gaps.root_cause: Encoding placed inside a per-chunk loop instead of batching.
gaps.impact: Ingestion of large document sets (100+ chunks) takes significantly longer than necessary. For 1000 chunks, ~50x slower than batch encoding.
gaps.fix: Batch encode: `texts = [c['text'] for c in all_chunk_objs]; all_embeddings = model.encode(texts, batch_size=64, show_progress_bar=True); all_embeddings = [e.tolist() for e in all_embeddings]`.

gaps.title: SMTP timeout not configured — email send can hang indefinitely
gaps.description: smtplib.SMTP(EMAIL_HOST, EMAIL_PORT) on L26 has no timeout parameter. If the SMTP server is unreachable, the connection attempt hangs indefinitely, blocking the agent's tool execution and freezing the UI.
gaps.root_cause: No timeout set on SMTP connection.
gaps.impact: Agent becomes unresponsive when email server is down. Streamlit UI freezes. FastAPI request times out with no response.
gaps.fix: Add timeout: `server = smtplib.SMTP(EMAIL_HOST, EMAIL_PORT, timeout=10)`. Also consider async email sending to not block the agent.

gaps.title: Uploaded filenames not sanitized in Streamlit
gaps.description: Line 69: `os.path.join(DOCS_DIR, f.name)` uses the uploaded filename directly. Similar to GAP-001, a file named '../../../.env' could write outside the documents directory.
gaps.root_cause: No filename sanitization on Streamlit file upload. Same vulnerability as the FastAPI upload.
gaps.impact: File write outside documents directory via Streamlit UI.
gaps.fix: Sanitize: `safe_name = os.path.basename(f.name); path = os.path.join(DOCS_DIR, safe_name)`.

gaps.title: No file size limit on upload endpoint
gaps.description: The upload endpoint reads entire file content into memory: `content = await f.read()` (L136). No size limit is enforced. A malicious user can upload a multi-gigabyte file to exhaust server memory.
gaps.root_cause: No Content-Length validation or streaming write for large files.
gaps.impact: Server OOM crash via large file upload. Denial of service.
gaps.fix: Add size limit: `MAX_SIZE = 50 * 1024 * 1024  # 50MB; content = await f.read(); if len(content) > MAX_SIZE: raise HTTPException(413, f'File too large. Max {MAX_SIZE} bytes.')`. Or use chunked streaming.

gaps.title: No duplicate message prevention on rapid submit
gaps.description: sendMessage() checks `if (!text.trim() || loading) return` but there's a race window: if the user double-clicks Send or presses Enter rapidly, the second call can execute before setLoading(true) takes effect (React state updates are async).
gaps.root_cause: React setState is asynchronous. Loading guard check races with state update.
gaps.impact: Duplicate messages sent to the agent, causing duplicate API calls and confusing conversation history.
gaps.fix: Use a ref for immediate synchronous check: `const isSubmitting = useRef(false); async function sendMessage(text) { if (isSubmitting.current || !text.trim()) return; isSubmitting.current = true; ... finally { isSubmitting.current = false; } }`.

gaps.title: No persistent conversation history in React frontend
gaps.description: The React frontend stores messages in useState, which is lost on page refresh. The Streamlit frontend has the same issue (session_state is per-tab). Neither frontend persists conversations.
gaps.root_cause: No localStorage, sessionStorage, or backend persistence for chat history.
gaps.impact: Users lose entire conversation history on page refresh, browser close, or tab switch.
gaps.fix: Add localStorage persistence: `useEffect(() => { const saved = localStorage.getItem('chat_history'); if (saved) setMessages(JSON.parse(saved)); }, []); useEffect(() => { localStorage.setItem('chat_history', JSON.stringify(messages)); }, [messages]);`.

gaps.title: No retry logic for OpenAI API calls
gaps.description: ChatOpenAI.invoke() on L122 has no retry logic. OpenAI API returns rate-limit errors (429), transient server errors (500, 503), and timeout errors. A single failure crashes the entire query.
gaps.root_cause: No retry/backoff wrapper around LLM calls.
gaps.impact: Intermittent query failures during peak API usage. User gets an error instead of a delayed response.
gaps.fix: Use tenacity: `from tenacity import retry, wait_exponential, stop_after_attempt; @retry(wait=wait_exponential(min=1, max=10), stop=stop_after_attempt(3)) def _invoke_llm(messages): return llm_with_tools.invoke(messages)`. Or use LangChain's built-in max_retries: `ChatOpenAI(..., max_retries=3)`.

gaps.title: No logging framework — debug prints to stdout
gaps.description: The entire codebase uses print() for logging (graph.py L22, L126, L128, L150; retrieval.py L33, L149, L242, L260; ingest_docs.py throughout). No log levels, no log rotation, no structured logging. Errors may go unnoticed.
gaps.root_cause: print() used instead of Python logging module.
gaps.impact: No ability to filter by severity, no timestamps, no log files for post-mortem analysis. Production debugging is very difficult.
gaps.fix: Replace all print() calls with logging: `import logging; logger = logging.getLogger(__name__); logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format='%(asctime)s %(name)s %(levelname)s %(message)s')`. Replace `print(f'...')` with `logger.info(f'...')`.

gaps.title: No pagination on documents listing endpoint
gaps.description: /api/documents lists ALL files in the documents directory via os.listdir(). With thousands of documents, this returns an unbounded response body.
gaps.root_cause: No limit/offset parameters on the endpoint.
gaps.impact: Slow API response and high memory usage with large document collections.
gaps.fix: Add pagination: `@app.get('/api/documents') def list_documents(skip: int = 0, limit: int = 50): ... files = files[skip:skip+limit]; return {'documents': files, 'total': total_count}`.

gaps.title: No rate limiting on chat endpoint
gaps.description: The /api/chat endpoint has no rate limiting. Each request triggers an OpenAI API call (~$0.01-$0.05). An attacker or buggy client can flood the endpoint, running up API costs and exhausting the OpenAI rate limit for all users.
gaps.root_cause: No rate limiting middleware configured.
gaps.impact: Financial cost from API abuse. Service degradation from OpenAI rate limits affecting legitimate users.
gaps.fix: Add slowapi: `from slowapi import Limiter; limiter = Limiter(key_func=get_remote_address); @app.post('/api/chat') @limiter.limit('10/minute') def chat(req, request: Request): ...`.

gaps.title: SourceTrackedRetriever uses get_or_create_collection — stale collection after ingest
gaps.description: The module-level _retriever (L208) creates a ChromaDB client and gets the collection on first import. After ingest_docs.py deletes and recreates the collection, the cached _retriever still holds a reference to the old (deleted) collection object. Queries after ingest may fail or return stale results.
gaps.root_cause: Module-level singleton holds a stale collection reference after ingest. FastAPI resets _graph but not the tools' internal retriever.
gaps.impact: After ingesting new documents, queries may return no results or stale results until the application is restarted.
gaps.fix: Add a refresh method: `def refresh_collection(self): self.collection = self.client.get_or_create_collection('hr_docs_v2')`. Call it from the ingest endpoint. Or make the collection access lazy: `@property def collection(self): return self.client.get_or_create_collection('hr_docs_v2')`.

gaps.title: PDF text extraction uses pypdf but preview uses pdfplumber — text mismatch
gaps.description: ingest_docs.py uses pypdf.PdfReader for extraction (L31), while document_viewer.py and retrieval.py search_policies use pdfplumber (L254). These libraries extract text differently (whitespace, line breaks, character encoding). Chunks stored in ChromaDB may not exactly match the text shown in preview, causing highlighting failures.
gaps.root_cause: Two different PDF libraries used for ingestion (pypdf) vs. preview/search (pdfplumber).
gaps.impact: Fuzzy matching fallback needed more frequently in _render_pdf_preview. Some chunks may never highlight correctly.
gaps.fix: Standardize on pdfplumber for both ingestion and preview: Replace pypdf usage in ingest_docs.py with `import pdfplumber; with pdfplumber.open(filepath) as pdf: for page in pdf.pages: text = page.extract_text()`.

gaps.title: AI-generated answer content injected as raw HTML without sanitization
gaps.description: answer_card_html(display_answer + refs_html) passes the LLM's response directly into HTML. If the LLM is prompt-injected to output HTML/JS (e.g., via a malicious document in the knowledge base), that code executes in the user's browser.
gaps.root_cause: LLM output treated as safe HTML via unsafe_allow_html=True.
gaps.impact: Indirect XSS via prompt injection. A malicious document uploaded to the knowledge base could cause the LLM to output JavaScript that executes in all users' browsers.
gaps.fix: Sanitize LLM output: `import html; safe_answer = html.escape(display_answer)` before passing to answer_card_html. Or use a whitelist-based HTML sanitizer that allows only formatting tags.

gaps.title: No loading indicator when opening source preview
gaps.description: togglePreview() calls generateHighlightedHtml() synchronously and then sets previewData. For large PDFs with fuzzy matching, this computation can take noticeable time (100ms+). During this time, the user sees no feedback after clicking 'Open'.
gaps.root_cause: No intermediate loading state between click and data display.
gaps.impact: UI feels unresponsive when opening large document previews. User may click multiple times.
gaps.fix:
Add loading state: `setPreviewData({type: 'loading'}); // Then in JSX: {previewData?.type === 'loading' &&
Loading preview...
}`. Use setTimeout or requestIdleCallback to defer heavy computation.

gaps.title: No .env.example file for developer onboarding
gaps.description: The .env file is gitignored but there's no .env.example with placeholder values. New developers must read config.py source code and architecture.xml to discover required environment variables.
gaps.root_cause: .env.example not created.
gaps.impact: Slower onboarding. Developers may miss optional variables like EMBEDDING_MODEL or RERANK_MODEL.
gaps.fix:
Create .env.example: `AI_KEY=your-openai-api-key-here
AI_MODEL=gpt-4o-mini
EMAIL_USER=your-email@gmail.com
EMAIL_PASS=your-app-password
EMAIL_HOST=smtp.gmail.com
EMAIL_PORT=587
# EMBEDDING_MODEL=all-MiniLM-L6-v2
# RERANK_MODEL=cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2`.

gaps.title: Manager stored as name string not foreign key
gaps.description: The employees table stores manager as a TEXT name (e.g., 'Priya Patel') not as an FK to employees.id. If a manager's name is updated, all references become stale. get_team() queries use LIKE matching which is fragile.
gaps.root_cause: Schema design choice — name-based reference instead of ID-based FK.
gaps.impact: Manager renames break org chart queries. Duplicate manager names could return wrong teams.
gaps.fix: Add manager_id INTEGER FK to employees table. Migrate data: `ALTER TABLE employees ADD manager_id INTEGER REFERENCES employees(id); UPDATE employees SET manager_id = (SELECT id FROM employees e2 WHERE e2.name = employees.manager)`. Update tools to join by ID.

gaps.title: Onboarding tasks only assigned to 3 of 20 employees
gaps.description: Onboarding records are only created for employee IDs 3, 16, and 20 (L115). The other 17 employees have no onboarding data. get_onboarding_checklist for any other employee returns 'No onboarding data found'.
gaps.root_cause: Seed data only covers a subset of employees for onboarding.
gaps.impact: Onboarding tools appear broken for most employees. Agent gives misleading 'no data found' responses.
gaps.fix: Assign onboarding tasks to all employees: `for emp in employees: for task in tasks: status = 'Completed' if emp[0] not in [3, 16, 20] else ('Completed' if task[0] <= 4 and emp[0] == 3 else 'Pending'); ...`.

gaps.title: reject_leave also has no status check — can reject already-approved leave
gaps.description: reject_leave() selects the leave record without checking status (L165-168). An already-approved leave can be rejected, but the balance deduction from approval is not reversed.
gaps.root_cause: Same as GAP-004 — no WHERE status='Pending' clause.
gaps.impact: Inconsistent leave state. Balance deducted by approval is never restored on subsequent rejection.
gaps.fix: Add status check: `SELECT ... FROM leaves WHERE id=? AND status='Pending'`. If status is 'Approved', return 'Cannot reject an already-approved leave. Consider revoking instead.'

gaps.title: Root package.json with duplicate dompurify dependency
gaps.description: The root package.json only contains dompurify as a dependency. This is also listed in frontend/package.json. The root package.json seems unnecessary and creates confusion about where to run npm install.
gaps.root_cause: Likely created accidentally or as a leftover from a previous setup attempt.
gaps.impact: Confusion for developers. Running npm install at root installs a duplicate dompurify in root node_modules.
gaps.fix: Delete root-level package.json and package-lock.json and node_modules folder. All Node dependencies should be managed in frontend/package.json.

gaps.title: Bare except clause swallows schema parsing errors
gaps.description: Line 42: `except: pass` — bare except clause in get_tool_descriptions() silently ignores any error when getting tool parameter schemas. This hides bugs in tool definitions.
gaps.root_cause: Defensive coding taken too far — blanket exception suppression.
gaps.impact: Tool parameter descriptions may be missing from the system prompt without any indication. Could affect LLM tool selection quality.
gaps.fix: Log the error: `except Exception as e: print(f'Warning: Could not get schema for {tool.name}: {e}')`.

gaps.title: Tenure calculation treats 0 days as no data due to falsy check
gaps.description: Line 47: `round(tenure_days / 365, 1) if tenure_days else 0` — if an employee joined today, tenure_days is 0 (or very close to 0). Python treats 0 as falsy, so the result is 0. While technically correct for 0 days, the check `if tenure_days` also catches None from NULL join_dates, creating ambiguity.
gaps.root_cause: Truthiness check used instead of explicit None check.
gaps.impact: Minor: same result for 0 tenure and NULL join_date. No functional difference in practice but semantically incorrect.
gaps.fix: Use explicit None check: `round(tenure_days / 365, 1) if tenure_days is not None else 0`.

gaps.title: source_preview.py is unused dead code
gaps.description: This file defines extract_citations, render_source_preview, render_side_by_side_layout, and render_compact_preview. None of these functions are imported or used anywhere in the codebase. The functionality has been superseded by document_viewer.py.
gaps.root_cause: Legacy code not removed after refactoring to document_viewer.py.
gaps.impact: Code confusion for developers. Maintenance burden for unused code.
gaps.fix: Delete utils/source_preview.py if confirmed unused: `git rm utils/source_preview.py`.

gaps.title: Empty rag/ package is unused placeholder
gaps.description: The rag/ directory only contains an empty __init__.py. All RAG logic is in tools/retrieval.py. This empty package adds confusion about where RAG code lives.
gaps.root_cause: Package created during initial planning but never populated.
gaps.impact: Developer confusion about code organization.
gaps.fix: Delete the rag/ directory: `rm -r rag/`. Or move retrieval.py into it for better organization.

gaps.title: Document count fetched once on mount — never refreshes
gaps.description: useEffect on L168 fetches /api/documents once on mount. After uploading new documents, the sidebar still shows the old count until the page is refreshed.
gaps.root_cause: No re-fetch trigger after upload or ingest operations.
gaps.impact: Stale document count in sidebar. Users may think their upload failed.
gaps.fix: Re-fetch after upload: Add a `refreshDocCount()` function and call it after successful upload or ingest.

gaps.title: React frontend missing document upload functionality
gaps.description: The Streamlit frontend has file upload and ingest buttons in the sidebar. The React frontend only has quick questions and clear button — no upload UI. The /api/upload endpoint exists but has no UI consumer.
gaps.root_cause: Feature not implemented in the React frontend.
gaps.impact: React users cannot upload new documents. Must use Streamlit or direct API calls.
gaps.fix: Add file upload section to the sidebar in App.jsx: `<input type='file' multiple onChange={handleUpload} accept='.pdf,.md,.txt,.docx,.csv,.xlsx' />`.

gaps.title: generateHighlightedHtml is not memoized — recomputes on every render
gaps.description: generateHighlightedHtml creates regex patterns and does fuzzy matching on every call. In togglePreview, it's called per-click which is fine. But it's also called per-line in snippet mode (L130) for the recursive highlighting pass, meaning a 50-line snippet triggers 50 regex compilations.
gaps.root_cause: Recursive call pattern inside the line-level rendering, with regex compilation inside the loop.
gaps.impact: UI jank when rendering large previews. Each line triggers full regex pipeline.
gaps.fix: Pre-compile regex patterns once and pass them down: `const patterns = sortedChunks.map(c => { try { return new RegExp(...); } catch { return null; } }).filter(Boolean);` Then pass patterns to the per-line call.
